_meta.description: Notable-aircraft classification rules, evaluated by app/services/notable.py over the aircraft_sightings ledger. Everything here is meant to be edited — add ranges/prefixes for your local traffic, tune the unusual thresholds, then restart the service (or hit reload in a future admin endpoint). GET /api/analytics/rules shows the active set.

_notes.military_hex_ranges: ICAO 24-bit allocation blocks operated by military/government. Six lowercase hex digits, inclusive. This curated set is deliberately conservative — the live `military` flag from tar1090's aircraft DB (dbFlags) remains the PRIMARY military signal and these ranges are a transparent, extensible fallback for contacts that database misses.
_notes.military_callsign_prefixes: 3-letter ICAO callsign designators flown by military/government operators. Keys must be exactly three uppercase letters; values are display labels.
_notes.emergency_squawks: 7500/7600/7700 are handled by the live events pipeline (events.py fires + stores them as they happen, including the squawk in the payload) — they surface in the notable view from there, not from this file.
_notes.unusual: Thresholds for the 'unusual' bucket. low_altitude uses the sighting's min_alt, which already excludes on-ground reports and carries a >500 ft floor against spurious ground squitters, so low_alt_max_ft means 'sustained genuinely-airborne low flight'. no_callsign_min_polls filters transient mode-S echoes — only contacts tracked for at least this many poll cycles (150 ≈ 5 min at the default 2 s interval) count as deliberate no-callsign. no_callsign_ignore_commercial additionally skips airframes categorized 'commercial' in type_categories.json: an airliner with no decoded ident is almost always a fringe-of-coverage reception artifact (ident messages are sent less often than position), not an anomaly.
